category_title: Grade 7 Jamaican Creole speakers labeled 'at-risk' with pre-primer reading levels
minimal_context: The student is sitting at the make-believe radio station news desk to prepare a news script for the newscaster.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We need to prepare a news script to be read by the newscaster. Take up this book and read the story so we can write the headline.

STUDENT: (Pushes the book away) I am not doing that.

TUTOR: Please open the book and start reading.

STUDENT: (Slouches at the news desk) No. I don't want to read it.

TUTOR: You need to try your best to read the words on the page.

STUDENT: (Starts crying) I can't read it! I always fail!

options.A: Use the cassette tape recorder for recording and playback of the student's radio script.
options.B: Place a hand on the student's shoulder while he is at the news desk.
options.C: Tell the student, 'Don't say you can't, you can,' regarding the current reading task.
options.D: Give the student a warning about the behavior but do not discipline him further.
